philippines.title: Free but deadly? Ex-convict kills fellow ex-convict in Dalaguete
philippines.description:
A 59-year-old former convict allegedly hacked to death his fellow ex-convict and longtime friend in Dalaguete town, southern Cebu, after the two figured in a heated confrontation inside the suspect’s house on Thursday afternoon, June 11.

philippines.title: Low chance of rains over weekend due to easterlies — Pagasa
philippines.description:
MANILA, Philippines — There is a low chance of rains across the country over the weekend due to the prevailing easterlies or warm winds from the Pacific Ocean, the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) said on Friday. In its weather bulletin, Pagasa said that only localized thunderstorms will be experienced, brought by cloud clusters in some areas. “The only weather systems affecting the country are the easterlies and localized thunderstorms.  This means, from Metro Manila up to the whole country, there is a low chance of rains that may cause widespread flooding,” weather specialist John Manalo said. READ: HEAT […]...
Keep on reading: Low chance of rains over weekend due to easterlies — Pagasa

philippines.title: Cebu Capitol marks 128th Independence Day by honoring Datu Lapulapu
philippines.description:
CEBU CITY, Philippines — The Cebu Provincial Government commemorated the 128th Philippine Independence Day on Friday, June 12, with a flag-raising ceremony and wreath-laying rites honoring Datu Lapulapu at the Capitol grounds. Governor Pamela Baricuatro led the ceremonies, which were attended by members of the Provincial Board, department heads, officials, members of the governor’s executive team, and Capitol employees. After the flag-raising ceremony, provincial officials gathered for a wreath-laying activity at the statue of Datu Lapulapu located within the Capitol complex. READ: HINDIPENDENCE DAY: Cebu protesters rally for ‘genuine national freedom’ Reflecting on Lapulapu, heroes’ sacrifices During her speech, Baricuatro […]...
Keep on reading: Cebu Capitol marks 128th Independence Day by honoring Datu Lapulapu

energy.title: India Accuses West of Double Standards Over U.S. Russia Oil Sanctions
energy.description: The on-and-off U.S. sanctions on Russian oil and the flipping U.S. position regarding India’s oil purchases from Russia highlight the double standards of the Western nations, Indian Foreign Minister S Jaishankar said on Friday. India turned en masse to Russian oil in 2022, when the U.S. and the EU imposed sanctions on Moscow due to the invasion of Ukraine. Four years later, India is a major buyer of Russia’s crude and Russia is India’s single-largest oil supplier. “At that time, the US specifically asked India to buy Russian…

energy.title: Oil Prices Slide as Traders Bet on Diplomacy
energy.description: July WTI crude oil futures traded sharply lower through Thursday as traders removed much of the geopolitical premium tied to tensions between the United States and Iran. Through June 11, the contract traded between a high of $95.47 and a low of $85.13 before settling near $85.93. At that level, prices were down $4.32, or 4.79%, from last week's close. The week's price action was driven largely by changing expectations surrounding President Trump's approach toward Iran. Early concerns that the conflict could escalate and threaten Middle East oil…
